_meta.purpose: Authoritative IFTA knowledge base the AI agent uses to ground answers. Updated quarterly when iftach.org publishes new rates.
primary_sources: California CDTFA online filing format (verified via MENSHIKOV LLC Q4 2025)

what_is_ifta.definition: International Fuel Tax Agreement — a cooperative agreement among the 48 contiguous U.S. states and 10 Canadian provinces that simplifies fuel-tax reporting for inter-jurisdictional motor carriers. Instead of fueling and paying tax in each state separately, the carrier files ONE quarterly return with their base jurisdiction, which redistributes funds to the others.
what_is_ifta.who_must_register: Any qualified motor vehicle (≥26,000 lbs GVW, OR 3+ axles regardless of weight, OR used in combination with combined weight >26,000 lbs) that operates in 2+ IFTA jurisdictions.
what_is_ifta.decals_and_license: Each qualified vehicle must display two IFTA decals (one per side of cab) issued by the base jurisdiction. License renews annually; decals are issued for the calendar year.
what_is_ifta.non_qualified_vehicles: Recreational vehicles (motor homes, pickup trucks with attached campers, buses used for personal pleasure) are exempt even if they cross jurisdictions.

filing_deadlines.deadline_rule: Postmark or electronic submission on or before the last day of the month following the quarter end. If the deadline falls on a weekend or holiday, it shifts to the next business day.
filing_deadlines.must_file_even_if_zero: A return MUST be filed each quarter even if the carrier did not operate or owes zero tax. Otherwise the license can be revoked.

penalties_and_interest.late_penalty: Greater of $50 OR 10% of net tax due, whichever is more.
penalties_and_interest.interest_rate: Set by IFTA, Inc. — currently 1% per month or partial month, applied to the unpaid balance per jurisdiction. Confirm current rate annually as it can change.
penalties_and_interest.license_revocation: Failure to file for two consecutive quarters or failure to pay can result in license revocation. Operating without an IFTA license while qualified to need one is a separate violation per state.

base_state_rules.definition: The single jurisdiction where the carrier is licensed for IFTA. Carrier files quarterly returns with this jurisdiction, which collects all tax and redistributes to other jurisdictions.
qualifications_to_be_base: Qualified motor vehicle(s) are registered there
Operational records are maintained or made available there
Some mileage is accrued in the base jurisdiction
base_state_rules.change_of_base: If you relocate your business, file a base-state-change request with both old and new jurisdictions. Cannot operate in two base states simultaneously.

fleet_mpg_calculation.formula: Total Miles ÷ Total Gallons across ALL trucks in fleet, ALL jurisdictions, for the quarter.
fleet_mpg_calculation.rounding: Rounded to 2 decimal places BEFORE per-state taxable-gallon calculation (verified via CDTFA filing).

sanity_range.note: Outside this band → likely a data error (missing miles, duplicate fuel entries, wrong units like km vs miles).

per_state_calculation.taxable_miles: Equal to Total Miles for most filers. Exempt miles (off-highway, agricultural, etc.) reduce this but require detailed records per state's exemption rules.
per_state_calculation.taxable_gallons: round(Total Miles ÷ Fleet MPG) — whole gallon, half-up.
per_state_calculation.tax_paid_gallons: Total gallons purchased in that state where state fuel tax was paid at the pump. Comes from fuel-card transaction records.
per_state_calculation.net_taxable_gallons: Taxable Gallons − Tax Paid Gallons. Positive = you owe additional tax, negative = state owes you a credit.
per_state_calculation.tax_due: round_half_up(Net Taxable Gallons × Tax Rate, 2). Sign convention: positive = owe state, negative = credit.
per_state_calculation.total_balance: Sum across all jurisdictions. Positive number = total tax owed to base state, negative = refund/credit.

surcharge_states._explanation: Some states impose a surcharge IN ADDITION to the base fuel tax. The surcharge is reported on a SEPARATE LINE on the IFTA return, calculated on TAXABLE GALLONS (not net), with no tax-paid credit (surcharge isn't collected at the pump).

KY.note: Kentucky base diesel + Kentucky Surcharge line. Surcharge applies to taxable gallons.

VA.note: Virginia base diesel + Virginia Surcharge line. Surcharge applies to taxable gallons.

surcharge_states.IN_not_surcharge: Indiana DOES have a 'SurChg' row in the IFTA matrix, but only for PROPANE (LPG) — not diesel. For diesel filers, Indiana's $0.6100 rate is reported as a single line with no separate surcharge.

OR.note: Oregon does NOT collect fuel tax through IFTA. It charges a Weight-Mile Tax (WMT) filed directly with ODOT Commerce & Compliance Division. IFTA rate for OR is 0.00, so 'tax_due' on the IFTA return for OR is always $0. However, miles in OR DO count toward fleet MPG, and gallons purchased in OR count toward tax-paid totals (just produce zero credit since rate is 0).

NY.note: New York charges a separate Highway Use Tax (HUT) on top of IFTA, for vehicles ≥18,000 lbs gross weight. HUT is filed quarterly with NY Department of Taxation and Finance and is INDEPENDENT of IFTA.

NM.note: New Mexico has a Weight Distance Tax (WDT) filed with NM TRD, for vehicles with declared gross weight ≥26,001 lbs. Separate from IFTA.
NM.agency: NM Taxation and Revenue Department — tax.newmexico.gov

KY_KYU.note: Kentucky Weight-Distance Tax (KYU) — separate from IFTA, separate from KY surcharge. For vehicles ≥59,999 lbs. Filed with KY Department of Revenue quarterly. KYU number is required for those vehicles in addition to IFTA decals.

non_ifta_jurisdictions.note: Alaska, Hawaii, Washington DC, Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut are NOT IFTA members. Miles in those jurisdictions are not reported on the IFTA return.

required_records: Distance records (per trip or per vehicle per jurisdiction)
Fuel receipts (paper or electronic) with date, location, gallons, price
audit_triggers: Unusually low or high fleet MPG
Tax-paid gallons that don't match fuel receipts
recordkeeping.auditor_authority: Base state can audit on behalf of all IFTA jurisdictions. Audits typically cover the 4-year retention window.

rate_matrix.update_cadence: Updated quarterly. Some states change rates mid-quarter — the matrix is authoritative.
rate_matrix.diesel_column: Special Diesel (column index 1, zero-indexed after jurisdiction+currency)

common_errors_and_fixes.MPG_too_low: Check for: (1) duplicate fuel rows, (2) gallons reported in liters/imp. gallons, (3) miles missing for one or more trucks.
common_errors_and_fixes.MPG_too_high: Check for: (1) duplicate mileage rows, (2) fuel purchased on a personal card not in the report, (3) miles double-counted between trip sheets and ELD.
common_errors_and_fixes.negative_state_miles: Should never happen. Indicates a sign error or correction-entry treated as data.
common_errors_and_fixes.fuel_in_state_with_no_miles: Possible if truck fueled at a border stop without entering the state. Verify the fuel-card location vs. ELD trace.
common_errors_and_fixes.missing_rate: If a state has miles but rate is 0, re-fetch the matrix (--refresh-rates). If still missing, the jurisdiction may be non-IFTA.
common_errors_and_fixes.wrong_base_state_in_PDF: The base state listed on the gov-portal cover sheet must match the carrier's IFTA license. Mismatched returns will be rejected.
common_errors_and_fixes.surcharge_omitted: If filing in California and KY or VA had taxable miles, you MUST add the separate 'Kentucky Surcharge' / 'Virginia Surcharge' line. Forgetting it leads to underpayment + interest assessment.

kentucky_dor_filing.agency: Kentucky Department of Revenue (KY DOR) — Motor Carrier Section
kentucky_dor_filing.ifta_portal_hint: Kentucky has its own e-file system. Carrier logs in with KY IFTA account credentials to enter the Jurisdiction Summary and submit.
kentucky_dor_filing.kyu_distinction: KYU = Kentucky Weight Distance Tax. SEPARATE from IFTA. Required for vehicles with declared gross weight >59,999 lbs that operate on Kentucky highways. Filed quarterly with KY DOR on its own form. Has nothing to do with the IFTA tax surcharge.
kentucky_dor_filing.ky_surcharge_for_home_state_filer: A KY-based fleet that runs in Kentucky has both a Kentucky base-tax line AND a Kentucky Surcharge line on its OWN home-state return. Do not omit the surcharge just because Kentucky is the base state — the surcharge applies to taxable gallons regardless.
filing_steps: 1. Login to KY DOR IFTA e-file system with carrier account
3. Enter Jurisdiction Summary line-by-line (or upload IFTA-standard CSV if supported)
4. Add Kentucky Surcharge line whenever taxable miles in KY > 0 (always true for KY-based fleet)
5. Add other surcharge lines (VA) if applicable
6. Verify computed total matches your pipeline total
7. Submit; save the filing confirmation PDF
8. Pay via ACH; save the payment confirmation PDF
9. If any truck >59,999 lbs, file the SEPARATE KYU return
kentucky_dor_filing.audit_oversight: Because Kentucky is the base state, KY DOR runs the IFTA audit (sampling all jurisdictions). Records must be available IN Kentucky or made available there on request.

cdtfa_california_filing.online_filing: Login → File a Return → IFTA Carrier Tax → enter the Jurisdiction Summary table line-by-line (or upload CSV in CDTFA format).
cdtfa_california_filing.payment: ACH debit, ACH credit, or credit card (with surcharge). Payment confirmation is separate from filing confirmation.
filing_steps: 2. Select the IFTA Carrier Tax account
3. Click 'File a Return' for the open quarter
4. Enter Jurisdiction Summary: one row per state, with Surcharge rows for KY and VA when applicable
5. Verify the system-computed total matches your pipeline total
7. Pay via ACH or credit card (separate confirmation)
8. Save both PDFs to MyTruck/ for the audit file

fuel_types.dyed_diesel: Off-highway / agricultural diesel (red-dyed). NOT subject to highway fuel tax — DO NOT report dyed-diesel gallons on the IFTA return. Highway use of dyed diesel is a federal violation.

exempt_fuel_and_miles.off_highway_miles: Miles driven off public highway (e.g. private yards, agricultural fields) may be claimed as exempt by some jurisdictions. Requires detailed mileage records per state's exemption rules.
exempt_fuel_and_miles.agricultural_use: Some states exempt agricultural-use miles. Requires written certification.
exempt_fuel_and_miles.fuel_tax_refunds: Fuel tax paid on exempt-use fuel (e.g. reefer fuel) can be claimed via the state's refund procedure — NOT on IFTA.
exempt_fuel_and_miles.rule_of_thumb: If unsure whether a mile is exempt, REPORT it. Conservative reporting reduces audit risk.

irp_vs_ifta.irp_definition: International Registration Plan — separate program for apportioned vehicle registration (license plates) based on miles per jurisdiction. Filed annually with base state.
irp_vs_ifta.ifta_vs_irp: IRP = vehicle registration based on miles. IFTA = fuel tax based on miles + fuel. Both apportion across jurisdictions but separate filings.
irp_vs_ifta.irp_filing_year: Calendar year. IRP renewal usually due Dec 31 of the prior year for the upcoming year.
irp_vs_ifta.common_misconception: Filing IFTA does NOT cover IRP. They are separate compliance obligations.

audit_process.audit_jurisdiction: Base state conducts the audit on behalf of all IFTA jurisdictions.
audit_process.audit_period: Usually 4 years (the record retention window). Can extend if fraud is suspected.
records_required: Distance records (per-trip or summarized; ELD/AOBRD logs preferred)
Fuel receipts (paper or electronic) with date, location, gallons, price
Mileage source documents (trip sheets, GPS logs)
Federal Form 2290 (Heavy Highway Use Tax) — confirms vehicle weight class
audit_process.audit_assessment: If records are incomplete, the auditor may impose an estimated MPG (typically 4.0 mpg — punitive) and reassess tax.
audit_process.appeal_window: 30 days from audit assessment notice. Each jurisdiction has its own appeal process.
common_findings: Missing fuel receipts → disallowed tax-paid credit
Incomplete mileage records → estimated MPG (usually higher tax)
Dyed-diesel reported on IFTA → reclassified, penalty
Surcharge omitted on KY/VA → assessed plus interest

ifta_license_year.decals: Two decals per qualified vehicle, one each side of cab. Display year matches license year.
ifta_license_year.renewal: Base state issues renewal applications in October–November for the upcoming year. Late renewals can incur penalties.
ifta_license_year.decals_expire: December 31. Vehicles operating in January without renewed decals risk citation.

common_filer_mistakes: Forgetting the KY or VA surcharge line when those states have taxable miles.
Filing Oregon at a non-zero rate (Oregon IFTA rate is $0 — weight-mile tax is separate).
Reporting dyed-diesel gallons as tax-paid fuel.
Skipping a quarterly return with zero activity (still must file).
Forgetting to separately file New York HUT, New Mexico WDT, Kentucky KYU when applicable.
Forgetting IRP renewal (different program, different deadline).
Pay-then-forget: payment confirmation is separate from filing confirmation on CDTFA — both must complete.
Reporting miles in non-IFTA jurisdictions (AK, HI, DC, YT, NT, NU) on the IFTA return.
